summary.intent: Fix issue #554 where local LLM server errors were swallowed and shown as generic 'Service temporarily unavailable', then improve LLM provider onboarding with a curated recommended tier and docs comparison page.
summary.outcome: Three-file bug fix merged via PR #559 (error code mapping, frontend error surfacing, model discovery timeout). Onboarding UI updated with recommended provider tier (including MiniMax and Z.AI/GLM), collapsible 'All providers' section, 'Help me choose' link, and new docs comparison page at docs.moltis.org/choosing-a-provider. PR review comments resolved with a fallback fix and clarifying comment.

repo: UNAVAILABLE error code is used directly in gateway/state.rs and methods/node.rs for node-connectivity checks — these bypass ServiceError and are unaffected by the From<ServiceError> impl change.

The just lint recipe excludes moltis-providers and moltis-gateway from --all-features and runs them separately with --features local-llm-metal on macOS; however --all-features on other workspace crates can still transitively pull in llama-cpp-sys-2 with CUDA, causing a pre-existing lint failure on macOS.
testModel() at provider-validation.js:104 already used res?.error?.serverMessage correctly, but validateProviderKey() at line 78 did not — an inconsistency introduced as a regression.
Provider onboarding sorts by uiOrder defined in known_providers; there is no backend 'recommended' flag — the curated tier was implemented frontend-only in onboarding-view.js.

Recommended providers selected: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Gemini, DeepSeek, MiniMax, Z.AI (GLM), Ollama — MiniMax and GLM 4.7 explicitly included based on user feedback about user happiness with those models.

code.finding: From<ServiceError> for ErrorShape mapped all non-Forbidden errors to UNAVAILABLE; changed to INTERNAL. Added comment explaining that UNAVAILABLE is reserved for transient node/gateway connectivity errors only.

validateProviderKey() used res?.error?.message (already localized) instead of res?.error?.serverMessage; fixed to prefer serverMessage with fallback chain. Also added a TODO/fallback for the case where serverMessage is absent.
fetch_models_from_api timeout increased from 8s to 15s to accommodate slow local LLM servers (llama.cpp, vllm) during model loading.

Provider list rendering split into two groups: RECOMMENDED_PROVIDERS set (frontend-only constant) shown first with descriptions, remaining providers collapsed behind a toggle that auto-expands if the active provider is non-recommended.

workflow: Running cargo test -p <crate> in parallel as background tasks significantly speeds up multi-crate verification.

When just lint fails with a pre-existing environment issue (CUDA on macOS), running clippy scoped to only modified crates (cargo clippy -p crate1 -p crate2) is a valid alternative to unblock the workflow.
Greptile bot posted automated review comments on the PR — these should be checked and resolved before merging.
mdbook build was run from within the docs/ directory, causing subsequent git commands to fail; absolute paths needed for git operations when cwd has changed.

friction: just lint fails with a pre-existing CUDA build error on macOS when --all-features transitively pulls in llama-cpp-sys-2; requires scoped clippy workaround.
Git commands failed after mdbook build changed cwd to docs/ — had to use absolute repo root paths.
Searching for KnownProvider struct and the available() serialization required multiple grep/read iterations before finding the right file sections.
open_items: Backend 'recommended' flag not added to KnownProvider struct — the curated tier is hardcoded frontend-only in onboarding-view.js; could be moved to backend metadata for consistency.
The docs comparison page (docs.moltis.org/choosing-a-provider) will need periodic updates as provider pricing, context windows, and model availability change.
Pre-existing just lint CUDA failure on macOS should be investigated and fixed separately — it masks real lint regressions.
Manual QA verification of the original #554 bug (unreachable endpoint showing real error instead of 'Service temporarily unavailable') was not performed in this session — left to human tester.
